payload.tactic: rewrite /inv_cpa; sp; if; auto; smt(mem_set get_setE).

last_result.result: EasyCrypt rejected the committed tactic. Use the error summary and current goal to revise the proof step.
last_result.effect: The manager attempted to commit a tactic; use the result and latest goal to decide what changed.
last_result.proof_state: The committed EasyCrypt proof state was not changed.
last_result.tactic: rewrite /inv_cpa; sp; if; auto; smt(mem_set get_setE).

inv_cpa SplitC2.I1.RO.m{1} SplitC2.I2.RO.m{1} Mem.log{1} Mem.log{2} Mem.
lc{1} Mem.lc{2} BNR.lenc{1} BNR.lenc{2} BNR.ndec{1} BNR.ndec{2} /\
(2.2)    Mem.lc <- if c0 \in Mem.log then Mem.lc else c0 :: Mem.lc
inv_cpa SplitC2.I1.RO.m{1} SplitC2.I2.RO.m{1} Mem.log{1} Mem.log{2} Mem.
lc{1} Mem.lc{2} BNR.lenc{1} BNR.lenc{2} BNR.ndec{1} BNR.ndec{2} /\

source.label: Parsed from EasyCrypt pretty goal text

moves.title: Residual closing tactic
moves.applicability: This tactic-shaped option should be checked with a read-only probe before committing it.
moves.effect: This is a read-only probe; it asks EasyCrypt what would happen without changing the committed proof state.
moves.derivation: read-only probe surfaced from proof-state analysis
moves.guarantee: read-only probe shape; proof state unchanged; probe then decide; reversible

frontier_alignment.summary: Frontier alignment: setup statements appear before the current frontier. Use this as a proof-reader map before choosing while/rnd/seq/call tactics.

first_instruction_alignment.right_first: EasyCrypt marks the programs as synchronized; the right-side statement is represented by the single residual program column.
first_instruction_alignment.proof_read: Only one side is at a branch frontier; consume or align the other side with `sp`, `wp`, or a seq cut before using `if`.

rows.left: 4 setup statement(s): p <- None; c0 <- c; p <- Mem.log.[c0]; ... (1 more)
rows.right: EasyCrypt marks the programs as synchronized; the right-side setup is represented by the single residual program column.
rows.proof_read: EasyCrypt marks the programs as synchronized here; this setup is shared residual program structure printed in one column, not an unmatched opposite-side obligation.

rows.right: EasyCrypt marks the programs as synchronized; the right-side statement is represented by the single residual program column.
rows.proof_read: EasyCrypt marks the programs as synchronized here; this row is shared residual program structure printed in one column. Use it to choose the next local proof step, not as evidence of a missing opposite-side statement.

rows.left: Mem.lc <- if c0 \in Mem.log then Mem.lc else c0 :: Mem.lc
rows.right: EasyCrypt marks the programs as synchronized; the right-side statement is represented by the single residual program column.
rows.proof_read: EasyCrypt marks the programs as synchronized here; this row is shared residual program structure printed in one column. Use it to choose the next local proof step, not as evidence of a missing opposite-side statement.

procedure_navigation.how_to_use: Positions for choosing wp/sp depth, which `if` to rcond, and where the next loop/call frontier is. Facts only — pick the tactic and the branch direction yourself.

left.note: count from frontier alignment; the body parser under-counted the asymmetric (right-only) setup statements (1 parsed)

absorb_depth.sp_hint: sp 4 0 / wp -4 -0 absorbs the straight-line setup (4 left, 0 right) before the next frontier

branch_guards.rcond_forms: rcondt{1} 2 or rcondf{1} 2 (direction needs guard entailment — yours to decide)

cut_position.tactic: seq 1 1 : (c2{1} = c1{2} /\ n{1} = n{2} /\ a{1} = a{2} /\ p0{1} = p0{2} /\ n{1} = p{1}.`1 /\ ! (p{1}.`1 \in BNR.lenc{1}) /\ inv_cpa SplitC2.I1.RO.m{1} SplitC2.I2.RO.m{1} Mem.log{1} Mem.log{2} Mem.lc{1} Mem.lc{2} BNR.lenc{1} BNR.lenc{2} BNR.ndec{1} BNR.ndec{2} /\ (forall (n0 : nonce) (c3 : C.counter), (n0, c3) \in SplitD.ROF.RO.m{1} => n0 \in p{1}.`1 :: BNR.lenc{1}) /\ p{1} = p{2}).

visible_key_cases.evidence: No equality/projection path to an update key is visible in this goal text.

obligation_families.effect: The pure goal contains finite-map update or lookup structure whose key/projection equalities are verifier-visible facts.
obligation_families.limitations: The surface does not rewrite any map expression.

obligation_families.effect: The remaining membership obligation relates an updated map key to a list or set membership fact.
obligation_families.limitations: The surface reports the alignment facts currently visible; it does not synthesize missing equalities.

obligation_families.effect: The current proof point is dominated by local logical obligations rather than program-frontier movement.
obligation_families.limitations: Quantifier names are not normalized across EasyCrypt pretty-printer variants.

translation_facts: Decorations identify the program memory from which a term is read.
Pretty-printer memory names and introduced proof-context names can differ.
ambient_memory_translation.effect: Local claims in the pure tail depend on the currently introduced memory binders as well as displayed term decorations.

membership_hypotheses: &1 (left ) : {c, c0 : ciphertext, p : plaintext option} [programs are in sync] &2 (right) : {c, c0 : ciphertext, p : plaintext option}
(n, c1) \in SplitD.ROF.RO.m{1} => n \in BNR.lenc{1} (1--) p <- None
(2.2) Mem.lc <- if c0 \in Mem.log then Mem.lc else c0 :: Mem.lc (2.3) p <- Mem.log.[c0]

visible_destructors.effect: Splits list membership into head and tail cases.
visible_destructors.limitations: The surface does not decide which case applies.

visible_destructors.effect: Keeps finite-map update keys visible beside membership obligations.

membership_decomposition_surface.effect: Summarizes list/map/filter membership sources whose local case facts are visible in the current pure tail.
limitations: No destructor tactic is chosen by this surface.
The panel does not assume which membership branch is true.

key_cases.evidence: No equality/projection path to an update key is visible in this goal text.

map_update_lookup_surface.effect: Summarizes finite-map update and lookup keys that determine same-key versus different-key local obligations.
limitations: This surface does not perform a rewrite or case split.
A key case marked unresolved only means no equality path is visible in this goal text.

pure_tail_surface.effect: Summarizes verifier-visible pure-goal structure after program frontier actions have been discharged or hidden by local proof steps.
limitations: This panel records syntactic and proof-state facts from the current view.
It does not choose a tactic or instantiate a lemma.

facts_and_diagnostics.fact_note: Only current-frontier facts are shown here; the full proof context artifact keeps the compiler/internal details.

notes.message: proof-state analysis sees no live call sites after a prior `inline *` at tactic 25. Continue at procedure/ambient layer, or undo if a call lemma is needed.

evidence: pure_tail_surface is visible for the current goal
current proof state has no program-frontier work before the logical residual

items: {'lookup_term': 'c0', 'case': 'key_case_unresolved', 'evidence': 'No equality/projection path to an update key is visible in this goal text.'}

related_checkpoints.why_checkpoint: seq-cut boundary; selecting it restores the proof state before this cut was committed

limitations: does not select a destructor, witness, rewrite, or SMT lemma
reported checkpoints are recovery references, not a default route

items.source: discarded suffix saved before checkpoint rewind

proof_piece_memory.interpretation: This is manager-owned evidence about prior route repairs and daemon-verified route options. Replayable pieces should still be verifier-checked before commit; stale and negative pieces are warnings, not tactics.

(p{1}.`1 \in BNR.lenc{1}) /\ inv_cpa SplitC2.I1.RO.m{1} SplitC2.I2.RO.m{1} Mem.log{1} Mem.log{2} Mem.lc{1} Mem.lc{2} BNR.lenc{1} BNR.lenc{2} BNR.ndec{1} BNR.ndec{2} /\ (forall (n0 : nonce) (c3 : C.counter), (n0, c3) \in SplitD.ROF.RO.m{1} => n0 \in p{1}.`1 :: BNR.lenc{1})).

lineage_briefing.interpretation: Manager-owned continuity notes for this node. Use them as resume/navigation evidence; EasyCrypt replay still decides whether any old route piece is valid.

inspect_lookup_handles.effect: All handles here are read-only manager requests; the committed EasyCrypt proof state stays unchanged.
inspect_lookup_handles.manager_note: Some requests may ask EasyCrypt or name resolution; wait for the manager result before choosing the next proof intent.

ask_manager_for.use_when: The latest transition recorded an error.
ask_manager_for.returns: manager-provided read-only context for this proof route

ask_manager_for.use_when: Need parsed goal shape, resolved names, or KB pattern hints.
The visible frontier contains call sites or named equiv handles may apply.
ask_manager_for.returns: call-ready/oracle-equiv context for the current frontier, plus the BODY of the procedure(s) called here (functor aliases resolved) so you can see which oracles/state your call invariant must preserve

ask_manager_for.use_when: You are at an abstract-adversary `call (_: <inv>)` and want the mechanical glob frame of the invariant before adding your own semantic conjuncts.
ask_manager_for.returns: the mechanical `={glob ...}` frame of the call invariant (shared abstract oracle modules, daemon-confirmed to apply and spawn the obligations). When a named coupling predicate fits the in-scope state it ALSO surfaces a field-threaded carrier (`inv … s1{1} s2{2}`), plus candidate inductive side-conditions (`dom ⊆ used-set`) for any random oracle the call queries. All revisable starting points — confirm the coupling and add/adjust the semantic conjuncts (key/state correspondences, set-membership).

ask_manager_for.use_when: You are considering `call (_: Inv)` and already have a concrete invariant expression you want to test first.
ask_manager_for.returns: a read-only preview of the obligations caused by that candidate invariant, including pre/init, call-preservation, oracle-side, postcondition, missing-fact signals, and extra conjuncts that may create avoidable obligations

ask_manager_for.use_when: A tactic has multiple EasyCrypt argument forms.
ask_manager_for.returns: valid tactic forms and common traps

ask_manager_for.use_when: The frontier may need indexed `sp i j` before branch or call tactics.
Mid-proof pRHL suffix surgery may need indexed `wp`.
ask_manager_for.returns: valid indexed `wp` forms and suffix-alignment traps

ask_manager_for.use_when: Statement order may need a small `swap` before `sp`, `wp`, or `sim`.
A guarded branch may need `rcondt` after a case split or invariant fact.
A guarded branch may need `rcondf` after a case split or invariant fact.
A suffix proof may need `conseq` to weaken to a smaller relation before `sim`.
ask_manager_for.returns: valid `conseq` forms and pRHL memory-annotation traps

ask_manager_for.use_when: One side may have an extra sample or need an explicit sample coupling.
A known statement-order mismatch may need an eager/lazy transformation.
ask_manager_for.returns: valid `eager` forms and when to prefer smaller surgery

ask_manager_for.use_when: LHS/RHS statement order may need swap/alignment context.
ask_manager_for.returns: swap/alignment context for the visible pRHL frontier

items.label: Restore before last checkpoint rewind
items.why_checkpoint: state saved before the last checkpoint rewind; selecting it returns to that proof state
items.effect_if_selected: This restores the branch to the proof state saved before the last checkpoint rewind.

items.label: Before inline expansion #25
items.why_checkpoint: inline boundary; selecting it restores the proof state before this expansion
items.effect_if_selected: Undo committed tactic #25 and every committed tactic after it in this node.

items.label: Return to resume start
items.committed_tactic: exists* (n{1}), (SplitC2.I1.RO.m{1}), (SplitC2.I2.RO.m{1}); elim* => n0 mr0 ms0; call (equ_cc n0 mr0 ms0); skip; move=> &1 &2; rewrite /check_plaintext /valid_topol /=; smt().
items.why_checkpoint: resume boundary; selecting it preserves the verified replay prefix and removes tactics committed after resume
items.effect_if_selected: Undo committed tactic #12 and every committed tactic after it in this node.

items.label: After call opened / before obligation work #6
items.why_checkpoint: call-local boundary; selecting it keeps the call tactic and removes work committed after the call opened
items.effect_if_selected: Undo committed tactic #6 and every committed tactic after it in this node.

items.label: Before call invariant #5
items.committed_tactic: call (_: inv_cpa SplitC2.I1.RO.m{1} SplitC2.I2.RO.m{1} Mem.log{1} Mem.log{2} Mem.lc{1} Mem.lc{2} BNR.lenc{1} BNR.lenc{2} BNR.ndec{1} BNR.ndec{2} /\ (forall n c, (n,c) \in SplitD.ROF.RO.m{1} => n \in BNR.lenc{1})).
items.why_checkpoint: call invariant introduction point; later oracle/branch obligations often reveal missing preserved facts or an over-strong equality from this tactic
items.effect_if_selected: Undo committed tactic #5 and every committed tactic after it in this node.

items.label: Before seq cut #11
items.committed_tactic: seq 1 1 : (c2{1} = c1{2} /\ n{1} = n{2} /\ a{1} = a{2} /\ p0{1} = p0{2} /\ n{1} = p{1}.`1 /\ ! (p{1}.`1 \in BNR.lenc{1}) /\ inv_cpa SplitC2.I1.RO.m{1} SplitC2.I2.RO.m{1} Mem.log{1} Mem.log{2} Mem.lc{1} Mem.lc{2} BNR.lenc{1} BNR.lenc{2} BNR.ndec{1} BNR.ndec{2} /\ (forall (n0 : nonce) (c3 : C.counter), (n0, c3) \in SplitD.ROF.RO.m{1} => n0 \in p{1}.`1 :: BNR.lenc{1}) /\ p{1} = p{2}).
items.why_checkpoint: seq-cut boundary; selecting it restores the proof state before this cut was committed
items.effect_if_selected: Undo committed tactic #11 and every committed tactic after it in this node.
